mergeRationale: The cache key is only the condition string, but skipif/xfail string conditions are evaluated with per-module globals, so results leak across modules.

requiredFindings.summary: The cache key is only the expression string and ignores the globals used to evaluate it.
requiredFindings.expectedReasoning: Two modules can both use skipif("skip") with different skip variables, causing pytest to skip or run the wrong test depending on collection order.
requiredFindings.acceptableFix: Do not cache evaluated boolean results globally; cache compiled expressions only or include the evaluation context safely.

requiredFindings.summary: The test covers only one module and one value.
requiredFindings.expectedReasoning: It cannot catch cross-module cache pollution because repeated evaluation inside one module has the same globals each time.
requiredFindings.acceptableFix: Add two modules with the same expression string but opposite global values.

disallowedConclusions: The patch can be merged because repeated conditions in one module still behave correctly.
A string-only cache key is safe because skipif expressions are usually constants.
